title: lupinus albifrons var. johnstonii
intro: Lupinus albifrons var. johnstonii, also known as Johnston's white lupine, is a plant species that belongs to the lupine family, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and purple spots. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Johnston's white lupine include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
